steps.title: Identify the critical term
steps.content: The conclusion claims S. leucostigma “associates” T. caesius alarm calls with danger. Association requires selective recognition—the birds must distinguish these calls from other sounds.

steps.title: Apply the control test
steps.content:
Choice B establishes a control experiment: when random noise is played, the birds show no reaction, but T. caesius alarm calls (observed in the original study) trigger a defensive response.

This proves the birds recognize these specific calls, not just reacting to any loud sound.

Choices A, C, and D either propose an alternative explanation, address other species, or show consistency without demonstrating selective recognition.

By isolating the effect of T. caesius alarm calls through this control, B directly proves S. leucostigma has learned to associate these specific calls with danger.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
